sections.paragraph:
στιβαρός, ά, όν (prob. cogn. with στείβω ‘tread upon’; Hom. et al.; IAndrosIsis 170; Ezk 3:6; Herm. Wr II, 2 [Sc. 134]; Jos., Bell. 6, 161; 293; SibOr Fgm. 3, 39) stout, sturdy (w. ἰσχυρός) δύναμις Hm 5, 2, 3.—DELG s.v. στείβω A 4.

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

IAndrosIsis IAndrosIsis = Der Isishymnus von Andros und verwandte Texte—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 945
